name: Is Perplexity's 'fair use' defense likely to win against content creators?
description: Weak if substituting your work; market harm key.[1]

mainEntity.name: Is Perplexity's 'fair use' defense likely to win against content creators?

acceptedAnswer.text: Weak if substituting your work; market harm key.[1]
acceptedAnswer.description: Unlikely if outputs substitute for originals without transformation, per NYT arguments beyond snippets.[1] Courts weigh four factors: purpose, nature, amount, market effect—AI substitution fails the last.[1] Key risks: ongoing cases fluid, but hallucinations weaken fair use.[1][3] Monitor rulings; prepare counterarguments on commercial harm. Strong facts favor creators.
